title: Notification reliability
subtitle: Make sure DosePulse reminders fire on time
intro: Your phone may put DosePulse to sleep in the background to save battery. That can stop scheduled reminders from firing. Here's how to whitelist DosePulse on your specific phone:

samsung.label: Samsung Galaxy
samsung.note: Also confirm DosePulse is not in 'Sleeping apps' or 'Deep sleeping apps'.

xiaomi.label: Xiaomi / Redmi / POCO
steps: Optional: long-press DosePulse in recent apps and tap the lock icon

huawei.label: Huawei / Honor
steps: Enable Auto-launch, Secondary launch, and Run in background

pixel.label: Google Pixel
pixel.note: Pixel is usually fine without changes. You can also exclude DosePulse from Adaptive Battery if you don't open the app daily.

fallback.label: Most Android phones
fallback.note: If your phone has its own battery manager (Motorola, Nokia, ASUS, etc.), look for an option to allow DosePulse to run in the background or auto-start.

checklist: Notification permission is granted (Settings → Apps → DosePulse → Notifications → On)
Exact alarms permission is granted (Settings → Apps → DosePulse → Special access → Alarms & reminders) — required on Android 12+
Do Not Disturb isn't on at the slot time
The supplement or activity is active (not paused) and today's weekday is in its schedule
